batch_summary: Deed [123] PDF pulled and analyzed (LC Vol. 48, Pg. 373, Inst# DR-00048-00373). CRITICAL FINDING: This is NOT a 1906 instrument — it is a warranty deed dated August 3, 1869, conveying 200 acres of the Sarah McAnulty League from James H. Bounds and wife Margaret E. Bounds to H. Clay Dunbar for $300. The 1906 index date was a RE-FILING (originally recorded Book H pp. 177–178, March 23, 1870). MAJOR DISCOVERY: The deed identifies Thomas Hare as the upstream source of JH Bounds' McAnulty holdings ('conveyed by Thomas Hare and wife to the said James H. Bounds'), a previously unknown acquisition almost certainly the '278 acre survey' source deed sought in ISSUE-34. The 200-acre Dunbar sale disproves the prior 137.75-acre speculation — the sale predates the 1906 partition by 37 years. Total documented McAnulty dispositions now 425.25+ ac. ISSUE-34 priority upgraded; ISSUE-40 item [123] marked PULLED. NOTE: Filename uses deed number [300] but this instrument is already tracked as [123] — manifest uses [123] per existing assignment.

02_PROPERTY_INVENTORY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [123] (Dunbar, 1869) — 200 ac McAnulty disposition added; Hare→Bounds upstream acquisition identified; gap analysis revised (total McAnulty dispositions 425.25+ ac)

operations.description: Update gap analysis item 1: replace speculative 'Original JH Bounds purchase or patent' with confirmed Hare→Bounds identification from deed [123]
operations.find: 1. **Original JH Bounds purchase or patent** — JH Bounds may have acquired the bulk of the McAnulty acreage directly from the state or from an earlier owner in a deed predating 1878 (the earliest deed in the [101]–[118] set). Deed [103] references "J. H. Bounds 278 acre survey" as a neighbor call — if JH Bounds held a 278-acre tract on or near the McAnulty League, that single acquisition could explain most of the gap. This "278 acre survey" has no source deed in the file set.
operations.replace_with: 1. **Thomas Hare → JH Bounds (pre-1869) — NOW IDENTIFIED as upstream source**: Deed [123] (1869-08-03, JH Bounds → H. Clay Dunbar, 200 ac) describes its parcel as "a part of" the McAnulty League "conveyed by Thomas Hare and wife to the said James H. Bounds." The Hare→Bounds conveyance is almost certainly the "278 acre survey" source deed referenced as a neighbor call in deed [103] (1881). Total Hare→Bounds acreage unknown but exceeds 200 ac (the Dunbar carve-out was the south 200 ac of the larger Hare→Bounds survey). Recording citation not stated in deed [123] — **action: search LC grantee index under JH Bounds pre-1869 to locate the Hare→Bounds deed**. This single acquisition likely accounts for the bulk of JH Bounds' McAnulty holdings.

operations.description: Add deed [123] analysis note after the '361 acres tract' paragraph in the McAnulty disposition section
operations.anchor: The [111] "361 acres tract" is the most significant new data point in the gap analysis. See ISSUE-34.
operations.content: **Deed [123] findings (2026-03-31)**: The Dunbar deed is now pulled. It conveys **200 acres** (NOT the speculated 137.75 ac) and is dated **August 3, 1869** — 37 years before the 1906 partition. The 08/16/1906 index date was a RE-FILING of an instrument originally recorded Book H pp. 177–178 (03/23/1870). The deed describes the 200 ac as "a part of" the McAnulty League "conveyed by Thomas Hare and wife to the said James H. Bounds" — identifying the previously unknown upstream Hare→Bounds source deed (see item 1 in gap analysis below). Because the 200 ac was sold in 1869, it was NOT part of JH Bounds' McAnulty holdings during the 1881 "278 acre survey" reference or the 1906 partition. The 137.75-acre gap within the 1906 partition (361 − 223.25) remains unexplained by instruments in the current file set — the Dunbar 200 ac does not bear on it. Total documented McAnulty dispositions: 200 (Dunbar, 1869) + 2 (church, 1887) + 223.25 (1906 partition) = **425.25 ac**, establishing JH Bounds' McAnulty holding as substantially larger than previously understood. See revised ISSUE-34.

operations.description: Replace deed [123] Phase 1 Supplement stub entry with full PDF-confirmed entry
operations.find:
#### Deed [123] — J. H. Bounds et al. → Dunbar H. Clay et al. (1906-08-16, Limestone)
- **Transaction type**: Deed — Sarah McAnulty League (acreage unknown from index)
- **Citation**: LC Vol. 48, Pg. 373, Inst# DR-00048-00373
- **Property**: Sarah McAnulty League, Limestone County (exact tract and acreage unknown)
- **Significance**: Pre-partition McAnulty disposition — recorded two months before the coordinated October 1906 family partition (deeds [111] and [22]). May explain part of the ISSUE-34 gap between the "361 acres tract" reference and the 223.25-acre partition total. If Dunbar deed acreage ≈ 137.75 ac: 223.25 + 137.75 = 361 ac — would close the gap exactly.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED OUT OF DIRECT CHAIN** pending confirmation. Pull PDF for acreage and tract description.
- **Action needed**: Pull LC Vol. 48 Pg. 373. Update ISSUE-34 arithmetic with confirmed acreage.
operations.replace_with:
#### Deed [123] — J. H. Bounds and wife Margaret E. Bounds → H. Clay Dunbar (1869-08-03, Limestone) — **PDF CONFIRMED**
- **Transaction type**: Warranty deed — surface + minerals, Sarah McAnulty League, 200 acres
- **Citation**: LC Vol. 48, Pg. 373, Inst# DR-00048-00373 (RE-FILING — see recording history below); original recording: Book H, Pp. 177–178, Limestone County, recorded 03/23/1870
- **Property**: 200 acres off the south end of the Hare→Bounds survey, Sarah McAnulty League, Limestone County. Beginning 26½ vrs from the SE corner of said McAnulty League, surveying on the south boundary line to a stake, thence north so as to include 200 acres, thence east to the line of said Hare→Bounds survey, thence south to place of beginning.
- **Consideration**: $300 cash
- **Upstream source**: "conveyed by Thomas Hare and wife to the said James H. Bounds" — the Hare→Bounds deed is a previously unidentified upstream McAnulty acquisition and is almost certainly the "278 acre survey" source deed sought in ISSUE-34. Recording citation for Hare→Bounds NOT stated in this instrument.
- **Parties**: Grantor: James H. Bounds and Margaret E. Bound[s] (wife). ⚠️ Name variation: deed body and signatures spell "Bound" (without s); acknowledgment uses "Bounds." Grantee: H. Clay Dunbar (Limestone County). ⚠️ Index entry "BOUNDS JAMES H +" and "DUNBAR H CLAY +" — the "+" indicators are misleading; there are no additional grantors beyond wife and no additional grantees.
- **Witnesses**: B. D. Loper, John L. Wolfe. ⚠️ "John L. Wolfe" — Margaret Emily Woolf/Wolfe was JH Bounds' wife; this witness may be a relative.
- **Acknowledgment**: November 13, 1869, before J. B. Vallandingham, County Clerk, Limestone County, at Springfield (former county seat). Margaret E. Bounds examined separate and apart from husband; acknowledged voluntary signing without compulsion. Revenue stamp 50 cents.
- **Recording history**: (1) Originally recorded Book H, Pp. 177–178, Limestone County, March 23, 1870, 11:00 AM, by J. B. Vallandingham, County Clerk. (2) **Re-filed** August 16, 1906, 8:00 AM; re-recorded August 27, 1906, 5:30 PM, by Alf Willis, County Clerk (Deputy: Ollie V. Jackson). The 1906 re-filing explains the index date of 08/16/1906 — the instrument is actually 37 years older.
- **Significance**: (a) **Earliest documented JH Bounds McAnulty instrument** — predates all other McAnulty deeds in the file set by 9 years (next earliest is [101], 1878, which is Holloway Survey). (b) Identifies **Thomas Hare and wife** as the upstream source of JH Bounds' McAnulty League holdings — a major ISSUE-34 breakthrough. (c) At 200 ac, the Dunbar sale does NOT close the 137.75-ac "361 acres tract" gap (the prior speculation was that it might ≈ 137.75 ac) — the sale predated the 1906 partition by 37 years and was not part of the 1906 partition arithmetic. (d) Total documented McAnulty dispositions now = 425.25+ ac (200 Dunbar + 2 church + 223.25 partition), establishing JH Bounds' McAnulty holding as substantially larger than previously understood.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED — NO REVERSION.** Full warranty deed; no reversionary clause; land permanently left family chain in 1869.
- **1906 re-filing context**: The re-filing occurred two months before the coordinated October 1906 family partition (deeds [111] and [22]). Probable purpose: clear the title record by putting the 1869 Dunbar sale on the modern index so that the 1906 partition buyers (Dean, MS Bounds) could verify that the 200-ac south end was already sold and excluded from the partition tracts.
- **Action completed**: PDF pulled; ISSUE-40 item [123] marked PULLED. ISSUE-34 significantly revised.

operations.description: Add deed [123] analysis paragraph to ISSUE-34, after the Pierce surname update and before the Status line
operations.anchor: Does not alter the 361-acre gap arithmetic.
operations.content: - **Deed [123] analysis (2026-03-31)**: PDF now pulled. Deed [123] (LC Vol. 48, Pg. 373) is a **warranty deed dated August 3, 1869** — the index date of 08/16/1906 was a RE-FILING, not the original recording (originally Book H pp. 177–178, 03/23/1870). Instrument conveys **200 acres** off the south end of the McAnulty League from JH Bounds and wife Margaret E. Bounds to H. Clay Dunbar for $300. ⚠️ The prior speculation (137.75 ac closing the 361-acre gap) is **INCORRECT**: (a) the acreage is 200, not 137.75; (b) the sale occurred in 1869, 37 years before the 1906 partition — the Dunbar parcel was already gone and is NOT part of the "361 acres tract" reference or the 1906 partition total. **CRITICAL DISCOVERY — upstream source deed identified**: The deed describes its 200 acres as "a part of" the McAnulty League "conveyed by Thomas Hare and wife to the said James H. Bounds." This **Hare→Bounds conveyance** is a previously unknown upstream acquisition — almost certainly the "278 acre survey" source deed. Total Hare→Bounds acreage unknown but exceeds 200 ac (deed [123] carved the south 200 ac from the larger survey). Recording citation not stated in deed [123]. **Revised total documented McAnulty dispositions**: 200 (Dunbar, 1869) + 2 (church, 1887) + 223.25 (1906 partition) = **425.25 ac** over the full JH Bounds McAnulty tenure. **Action**: Search LC grantee index under JH Bounds pre-1869 for Hare→Bounds deed — this is now the **single most important missing upstream instrument** in the McAnulty chain. The 137.75-acre gap within the 1906 partition remains a separate question unresolved by the Dunbar deed.
